description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, natural appearance and a predominantly grayish-tan coloration. Its surface is uneven, featuring a mix of coarse, granular textures and smoother, weathered patches, suggesting exposure to the elements over time. The rock has several protrusions and indentations, with some areas appearing more angular and others more rounded, giving it a distinctly organic form. The coloration is mostly uniform but includes subtle variations in shade, with some lighter and darker spots that enhance its realistic look. The rock's size and mass make it suitable as a physical barrier or decorative element in an urban environment, such as a park or landscaped area. There are no visible markings, graffiti, or signs of human modification on its surface.
description: A rugged, grayish-tan rock with an irregular, coarse surface and natural weathering, suitable as an urban barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rock's elongated form, with prominent surface bumps and a mix of rough and smoother textures.
description_view_1: From this angle, the rock appears more compact, showing a blocky end with deep crevices and shadowed recesses.
description_view_2: This side reveals a concave area with pronounced indentations and a darker, more shadowed surface.
description_view_3: The top-down perspective emphasizes the rock's length and the granular, pitted texture covering much of its upper surface.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
